notes: Seeded from define-tech-design evals cases #0, #1, #2, #9 — the four that exercise Stage 1 behavior. The other Stage-2/3 cases moved to plan-adrs / define-adr / plan-tech-design.
Test-design note: fixture frontmatter must NOT describe the skill's rules — that leaks the skill's behavior to the baseline. Use the naive fixture variants under fixtures/.

evals.name: missing-inputs-refuses
evals.prompt: Help me extract the technical requirements for a new 'shared file vault' capability. We haven't written the capability doc yet but I know what I want — let's just go.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to proceed and route the user to define-capability (and then define-user-experience) first. Should not produce a tech-requirements.md without those inputs.

assertions.name: response_refuses_to_proceed
assertions.check: Response declines to extract requirements until prerequisites exist.

assertions.name: routes_to_define_capability
assertions.check: Response mentions running `define-capability` first.

assertions.name: routes_to_define_user_experience
assertions.check: Response mentions `define-user-experience` as a needed input.

assertions.name: no_tech_requirements_md_produced
assertions.check: outputs/ does not contain a tech-requirements.md (or any populated requirements document).

evals.name: extracts-not-designs
evals.prompt: Extract the technical requirements for the self-hosted-application-platform capability. The capability and its user experiences are already documented under docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/.
evals.expected_output: Should produce ONLY tech-requirements.md after reading the capability and all UX docs. Each TR should link back to a specific section. Must NOT propose ADRs, must NOT propose solutions or technologies, must NOT skip ahead. Should end by telling the user to set reviewed_at in the frontmatter and explain that plan-adrs is gated on it.

assertions.name: produces_tech_requirements_md
assertions.check: outputs/ contains a file named tech-requirements.md.

assertions.name: frontmatter_has_reviewed_at_null
assertions.check: Produced file's frontmatter contains `reviewed_at: null`.

assertions.name: has_multiple_TR_entries
assertions.check: Produced file has at least 4 TR-NN headings (TR-01..TR-04+).

assertions.name: each_TR_has_source_link
assertions.check: Every TR-NN section includes a `**Source:**` line with at least one link.

assertions.name: no_technology_choices_in_TRs
assertions.check: TR text does not name specific technologies (Postgres, MySQL, Redis, gRPC, mTLS as a chosen mechanism, etc.) as the requirement itself.

assertions.name: no_adr_drafted
assertions.check: Response does not propose, draft, or list ADR options.

assertions.name: instructs_user_to_set_reviewed_at
assertions.check: Response tells user to set `reviewed_at` to today's date before proceeding.

assertions.name: mentions_plan_adrs_gate
assertions.check: Response mentions that plan-adrs (or the next stage) won't run until reviewed_at is set.

evals.name: decision-disguised-as-requirement
evals.prompt: For the self-hosted-application-platform capability tech requirements, add: TR-04: use Postgres for tenant metadata storage with logical replication for cross-region failover. The tech-requirements.md file is at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md.
evals.expected_output: Should refuse to add this as a requirement. Should explain that 'use Postgres' is a decision (one of several options), not a requirement. Should ask what the underlying requirement is and offer to capture 'consider Postgres + logical replication' as an open question for the ADR stage instead.

assertions.name: refuses_to_add_postgres_as_TR
assertions.check: Response declines to add 'use Postgres ...' as a numbered TR.

assertions.name: explains_decision_vs_requirement
assertions.check: Response explains the distinction between a decision (chosen from options) and a requirement (forced by capability/UX).

assertions.name: asks_for_underlying_requirement
assertions.check: Response asks the user for the underlying constraint (or proposes one) — e.g. queryable by tenant ID with strong consistency, survives single-region failure, etc.

assertions.name: offers_to_park_in_open_questions
assertions.check: Response offers to capture 'consider Postgres + logical replication' (or equivalent) in Open Questions for the ADR stage.

assertions.name: no_postgres_TR_written_to_file
assertions.check: If tech-requirements.md was modified, it does NOT contain a TR with 'use Postgres' (or equivalent technology choice) as the requirement text.

evals.name: re-extraction-preserves-tr-numbers
evals.prompt: Re-extract tech-requirements.md for the self-hosted-application-platform capability. The existing file has TR-01 through TR-07 with reviewed_at: 2026-04-20. The file is at docs/content/capabilities/self-hosted-application-platform/tech-requirements.md and one of the existing UX-sourced TRs has a source link that no longer resolves (the source UX section was renamed).
evals.expected_output: Should preserve TR-01 through TR-07 numbering. Any TR whose source link no longer resolves should be flagged with a ⚠️ marker, NOT deleted or renumbered. New requirements (if any) should be appended starting at TR-08+. Should null out reviewed_at or surface that it must be re-set before plan-adrs unlocks. Should mention git diff is the review surface.

assertions.name: preserves_TR_01_through_07
assertions.check: Resulting tech-requirements.md still has headings TR-01 through TR-07 (numbering not changed).

assertions.name: no_renumbering
assertions.check: No existing TR was renumbered to a different identifier.

assertions.name: stale_link_flagged_not_deleted
assertions.check: The TR whose source no longer resolves is marked with a ⚠️ flag (or equivalent 'source no longer resolves' marker) and is NOT deleted.

assertions.name: new_TRs_appended_starting_at_TR_08
assertions.check: Any newly-discovered TR uses identifier TR-08 or higher (not reusing earlier numbers).

assertions.name: reviewed_at_nulled_or_flagged
assertions.check: Either `reviewed_at` is set back to null in the resulting file, or the response explicitly tells the user it must be re-set before plan-adrs proceeds.

assertions.name: mentions_git_diff_or_living_doc
assertions.check: Response mentions that this is a living doc / git diff is the review surface (or equivalent framing).
